name: Koenigsberger Klops (German Meat Balls)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine ground beef and pork (I used veal too), chopped onion, bread crumbs, 1 tsp of the salt, pepper, and nutmeg in a medium bowl until blended.
2. Separate eggs; put whites into a large bowl and yolks into a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate the yolks.
3. Beat egg whites until they form soft peaks; fold into meat mixture.
4. Shape into 1-inch balls and place in an electric slow cooker. (I browned these slightly in a skillet).
5. Combine beef broth, water, quartered onion, vinegar, sugar, pickling spices, and remaining 1/2 tsp salt in a small saucepan. Bring to boil, lower heat, and simmer for 15 minutes; strain into slow cooker; cover.
6. (Do the same: pour into skillet, reduce heat, cover).
7. Cook on low (190-200 degrees) for 5 hours; remove meat balls to a heated deep platter and keep warm.
8. Turn heat control to high (290-300 degrees). Combine 1 tbsp all-purpose flour with 2 Tbsp cold water in a cup; stir into liquid in cooker until smooth; cover.
9. Cook for 15 minutes.
10. Beat the saved egg yolks with a fork; beat in hot sauce; return to cooker; cook for 5 minutes.
11. Pour over meat balls.
12. Garnish with lemon slices and capers; serve with boiled parsley.
(Note: This recipe is from Sallie Krebs - Happy Cookin')
